laws.title: Public Law 4-31 | Stand Your Ground Act
laws.status_basis: Listed in the source manifest under 'Repealed Laws'. The source body itself contains no self-repeal or sunset; it enacts a permanent general criminal-defense scheme (justifiable use of force / castle-doctrine). Repeal is external, so status rests on the repealed-laws listing rather than internal text.

provisions.text_summary: Short title 'Stand Your Ground Act' / S. 31.
provisions.evidence: “This Act may be cited as the ‘Stand Your Ground Act’ or simply as S. 31.”
provisions.notes: Short title only.

provisions.text_summary: Purposes: protect citizens standing their ground from criminals and legal repercussion.
provisions.evidence: “It is the purpose of this act to— a. Protect citizens from armed criminals.”
provisions.notes: Purpose clause.

provisions.text_summary: Enactment/effective on passage and signature.
provisions.evidence: “This bill shall be enacted once passed through both chambers of Congress and signed by the President”

provisions.text_summary: Definitions: law enforcement agency/officer, dwelling, residence, vehicle.
provisions.exact_change: Would define terms for a self-defense/use-of-force chapter.
provisions.evidence: “SEC. 101. Definition (a) Law enforcement agency shall be defined as...”
provisions.notes: Substantive general law; placement at t50/s1 is wrong subject matter.

provisions.text_summary: Justifiable use of force; no duty to retreat in dwelling/residence.
provisions.evidence: “has no duty to retreat and has the right to stand his or her ground”

provisions.text_summary: Presumption of reasonable fear on unlawful/forcible entry.
provisions.evidence: “A person is presumed to have held a reasonable fear of imminent peril...”

provisions.text_summary: Exceptions to the presumption (lawful resident, criminal activity, law enforcement officer).
provisions.evidence: “The presumption set forth in section 103 of this title does not apply if:”

provisions.text_summary: Presumption that forcible entrant intends unlawful violent act.
provisions.evidence: “is presumed to be doing so with the intent to commit an unlawful act involving force or violence.”

current_implementation.detail: Note rp-pl004031-codification carries a full-text quotedContent dump and a Trello link, placed at t50/s1 (war/national defense) which is the wrong title — this is a general self-defense/criminal-law enactment properly belonging in title 18. Declared codification is boilerplate; the note substitutes for what should be excluded-from-code text (repealed) or a concise history note.

recommended_actions: Remove full-text dump and Trello link from rp-pl004031-codification.
Reclassify as fully-repealed; retain only a concise historical/statutory note (repealed), and correct placement out of t50 to a crimes/self-defense anchor (t18) if any note is kept.

laws.title: Public Law 4-32 | Warrants, Subpoenas, and Summons Act
laws.status_basis: The extracted law_txt_path is NOT the Warrants/Subpoenas/Summons Act itself — it is Executive Order 18-4 (a presidential directive) that merely references PL 4-32 as its authorizing statute. The operative statute text of PL 4-32 is not present in the supplied source, so no lawful codification conclusion about the Act's own provisions can be drawn.

provisions.text_summary: Findings/purpose: establishes DOJ-notification procedures for warrant requests; cites PL 4-32 as authority.
provisions.evidence: “The purpose of this executive order is to establish procedures for law enforcement when requesting a warrant... as they are authorised to do under Public Law 4-32”
provisions.notes: This is an Executive Order, not the public law. Executive orders are not codified as U.S. Code section text.

provisions.text_summary: Procedures for requesting warrants/subpoenas, DOJ notification timing, exigent-circumstances and reasonable-suspicion definitions with caselaw cites.
provisions.evidence: “Under Public Law 4-32... Law enforcement are authorised to request an arrest warrant, when there is probable cause...”
provisions.notes: Administrative directive; not statutory text of PL 4-32.

current_implementation.detail: Note declares 'Current or not wholly repealed' but the underlying source is an Executive Order, not the enacted PL 4-32 statutory text. Any codification note purporting to represent PL 4-32 rests on the wrong document. Trello link present; no dump.

recommended_actions: Do not codify EO 18-4 as PL 4-32 statutory text; retain at most a concise historical note flagging that the supplied source is an executive order referencing the Act, not the Act itself.
Flag source defect: locate the true PL 4-32 enacted text before any codification; remove Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 4-33 | National Archives Act
laws.status_basis: Listed under 'Repealed Laws'. Internally the Act is permanent and general (establishes NARA as an independent agency); no self-repeal or sunset. Repeal is external.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, immediate effective date, authorizes edit/copy of prior amended bills.
provisions.evidence: “This piece of legislation shall be referred to as National Archives Act of 2022”; “This act shall be severable”

provisions.text_summary: Establishes the National Archives and Records Administration as an independent federal agency, headed by the Archivist and four Co-Archivists; President nominates with Senate advice/consent.
provisions.exact_change: Establish NARA and the office of Archivist.
provisions.evidence: “The National Archives and Records Administration will be established as an independent federal agency.”
provisions.notes: General organic law; proper title placement (t44) but should be actual section text if retained, not just a note.

provisions.text_summary: Responsibilities: maintain records of laws, court decisions, elections; maintain Federal Register/CFR.
provisions.evidence: “The National Archives and Records Administration will keep an up-to-date record of all laws...”; “shall maintain the Code of Federal Regulations (Federal Register)”

current_implementation.detail: Full-text dump + Trello link present. Title placement (t44) is correct subject-matter, but the note is a full-text dump rather than a concise history note, and no real section text was created. Since the law is repealed, positive-law section text is not required, but the dump/Trello must be removed.

recommended_actions: Remove full-text dump and Trello link from rp-pl004033-codification.
Retain a concise historical note at title 44 recording enactment and repeal of the National Archives Act.

laws.title: Public Law 4-34 | An Act to Prevent the Proliferation of Magistrates and Ensure Basic Judicial Competence
laws.status_basis: Listed under 'Repealed Laws'. Internally a permanent general rule (bar-certification requirement for magistrate/judicial appointments); no self-repeal or sunset. Repeal external.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, effective on passage.
provisions.evidence: “referred to as ‘H.R. 4015, an Act to Prevent the Proliferation of Magistrates...’”

provisions.text_summary: No person may be appointed a Magistrate (excluding confirmed Judges/Justices) without federal-court certification; appointing officer liable to impeachment.
provisions.evidence: “No person shall be appointed as a Magistrate... without first being certified to practice in a federal court”
provisions.notes: Placement at t50/s1 (war/defense) is the wrong subject; belongs in judiciary (t28).

provisions.text_summary: Same certification requirement for Judges/Justices; appointing officer liable to impeachment.
provisions.evidence: “No person shall be appointed as a Judge or Justice... without first being certified to practice in a federal court”

provisions.text_summary: Persons already appointed get ten days to comply or face impeachment/removal.
provisions.evidence: “shall have ten days from its passing into law to become in compliance”
provisions.notes: Transitional applicability clause.

current_implementation.detail: Full-text dump + Trello link; placed at t50/s1 which is wrong (judiciary matter belongs in t28). Repealed law, so no section text required, but dump/Trello must be cleaned and placement corrected if a note is kept.

recommended_actions: Remove full-text dump and Trello link from rp-pl004034-codification.
Move any retained historical note out of t50 to title 28 (judiciary); mark as repealed.

provisions.evidence: “Google Drive - Can't download file ... the owner hasn't given you permission to download this file.”
provisions.notes: Source-limited; cannot enumerate provisions.

laws.title: Public Law 4-36 | Defense Heraldic Services Act
laws.status_basis: Listed under '1st - 10th U.S. Congress' (not repealed). The extracted HTML contains the full operative text (embedded in Google-Drive viewer chrome but readable). It makes express amendments to Title 10 with no self-repeal/sunset.

provisions.text_summary: Short title; immediate effective date; conflicting legislation null; severability.
provisions.evidence: “referred to as the ‘Defense Heraldic Services Act of 2022’”

provisions.text_summary: Amends Chapter 767 of Title 10 by REPEALING section 7594 in its entirety and reserving it.
provisions.exact_change: Repeal 10 U.S.C. §7594 (Secretary of the Army authorization to furnish heraldic services) in its entirety; mark reserved.
provisions.evidence: “Chapter 767 of Title 10... is amended– (1) by repealing section §7594 in its entirety, and rendering it reserved”
provisions.notes: Requires actual textual repeal-marking, not merely a note.

provisions.text_summary: Amends Subchapter II of Chapter 48 of Title 10 by adding a new section furnishing heraldic services under the Secretary of Defense.
provisions.exact_change: Insert new section (source labels it '§206') at end of subchapter II of ch. 48 with subsections (a) design of flags/insignia/etc. by DoD authority, (b) advising other agencies on heraldry, (c) reimbursement regulations.
provisions.evidence: “Subchapter II of Chapter 48 of Title 10... is amended– (1) by adding the new following section at the end thereof: ‘§206. Furnishing of heraldic services’”
provisions.notes: Source section number '§206' likely a drafting artifact; integrator must place it correctly within ch. 48 subch. II numbering. This is a real textual insertion, NOT a note.

current_implementation.detail: Correct title (t10) and note correctly says 'Operative amendments are integrated where determinable,' but the draft only added a note at t10/s101/h and did NOT perform the express textual amendments (repeal of §7594; insertion of new heraldic-services section in ch. 48 subch. II). Express amendments to numbered Code provisions must be actual textual amendments.

recommended_actions: Perform actual repeal-marking of 10 U.S.C. §7594 (reserve it) per SEC. II.
Insert the new heraldic-services section into Title 10, Chapter 48, Subchapter II per SEC. III with source-credit to PL 4-36; do not rely on the s101/h note.
Remove Trello link from rp-pl004036-codification.

laws.title: Public Law 7-42 | Transportation Act of 2022
laws.status_basis: Listed under 'Repealed Laws'. Internally permanent general (creates transit divisions, FTA, TSA); no self-repeal/sunset (contains a repeal of another measure, not itself). Repeal external.

provisions.text_summary: General provisions: short title (declines to endorse), effective date, severability, conflicting-legislation null.
provisions.evidence: “referred to as the ‘Transportation Act of 2020’. No short title is endorsed by Congress.”
provisions.notes: Source text says 2020; PL titled 2022 — likely reused/backdated draft.

provisions.text_summary: Findings/whereas clauses on transportation needs.
provisions.evidence: “WHEREAS; Vehicles in this country tend to cause significant delay...”

provisions.text_summary: Establishes a Bus Rapid Transit Division under DOT.
provisions.evidence: “There shall be a Bus Rapid Transit Division under the Federal Government, specifically the Department of Transportation”

provisions.text_summary: Forms the Federal Transit Administration under DOT with a Director and duties.
provisions.evidence: “The Federal Transit Administration is hereby formed under the direction of the Department of Transportation”

provisions.text_summary: Establishes the Transportation Security Administration under DOT; air-traffic regulation authority.
provisions.evidence: “The Transportation Security Administration is hereby established under the direction of the Department of Transportation”

provisions.text_summary: Regulations; municipal-law prohibition repealing conflicting municipal transit/towing laws pursuant to D.C. Home Rule Act (Pub. L. 12-9); deprivatization; general counsel. Also strikes/repeals 'S 63 Towing Companies Regulation Act.'

provisions.evidence: “S 63 Towing Companies Regulation Act is hereby struck down and repealed.”; “pursuant to the District of Columbia Home Rule Act of 2015, Pub. L. 12-9.”
provisions.notes: Cites Pub. L. 12-9 (a later-numbered PL) as authority — anachronistic cross-reference in source; flag for integrator.

provisions.text_summary: Municipal authority over routes/towing/airspace recommendations.
provisions.evidence: “the Municipal Government shall entertain the authority to establish transportation regulations”

provisions.text_summary: Development suggestions (expressly not law).
provisions.evidence: “This section should not be construed to be the law of the United States, but the unanimous suggestion...”
provisions.notes: Self-identified non-operative.

current_implementation.detail: Full-text dump + Trello link at t49 (correct subject). Repealed law; dump/Trello must be removed. The internal repeal of 'S 63 Towing Companies Regulation Act' and reliance on Pub. L. 12-9 should be captured in a history note only, not as new section text.

recommended_actions: Remove full-text dump and Trello link from rp-pl007042-codification.
Retain a concise historical note in title 49 (repealed); flag anachronistic cross-reference to Pub. L. 12-9 and the repeal of S. 63 for the integrator.

laws.title: Public Law 7-43 | District of Columbia Reorganization Initiative Act
laws.status_basis: Listed under 'Repealed Laws'. Purely local/District measure amending the D.C. City Charter; no self-repeal/sunset. Repeal external.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, effective date; anti-supersession clause.

provisions.text_summary: Amends Article I of the D.C. City Charter (Council membership from six to five members; abolishes Section IV of Article I).

provisions.notes: Amends the D.C. Charter, not the U.S. Code — local, not codifiable in USC.

provisions.text_summary: Establishes D.C. line of succession for the office of Mayor.
provisions.evidence: “The Line of Succession for the Office of the Mayor of Washington, DC shall be the following...”

current_implementation.detail: Full-text dump + Trello link at t40. Note correctly recognizes local character ('local, appropriations, individual...'), but retains a full dump. This is a purely local/District Charter amendment — not U.S. Code section text — and is repealed.

recommended_actions: Remove full-text dump and Trello link from rp-pl007043-codification.
Exclude from permanent Code (local/District Charter amendment, repealed); keep at most a concise historical note.

laws.title: Public Law 9-53 | Save Our Businesses Act of 2022
laws.status_basis: Listed under 'Repealed Laws'. Internally makes express repeals and amendments (including a direct amendment to 3 U.S.C. §301) and contains sunset-tagged detected language; no self-repeal but repealed externally. Its own amendments would need unwinding upon its repeal.

provisions.text_summary: Short title (Save Our Businesses Act of 2022).
provisions.evidence: “referred to as the ‘Save Our Businesses Act of 2022.’”

provisions.text_summary: Repeals Public Law 7-40 (Media Protections Act) and Public Law 3-22 (District of Columbia Administration Act).
provisions.exact_change: Repeal PL 7-40 and PL 3-22 in their entirety.
provisions.evidence: “Public Law 7-40, the Media Protections Act is repealed. Public Law 3-22, the District of Columbia Administration Act is repealed.”
provisions.notes: Confirms fully-repealed status of PL 7-40 (this batch).

provisions.text_summary: Regulatory power of the Department of Commerce (draft policy, notify businesses, delegate authority, investigate, refer violations, require reporting).
provisions.exact_change: Grant Commerce regulatory/enforcement powers over business licensing.
provisions.evidence: “The Secretary shall within 3 business days of passage: Draft sufficient policy, or issue regulations...”

provisions.text_summary: Contract negotiations for issuance/renewal of business licenses; publication; optional arbitration.
provisions.evidence: “Prior to the issuance, or renewal of a business license or registry, the Secretary shall: Negotiate a contract...”

provisions.text_summary: EXPRESS AMENDMENT of 3 U.S.C. §301 (General authorization to delegate functions) — amends it to read a new expanded delegation text (adds authority to establish departments/agencies, suspend/transfer statutory authorities and personnel, transfer Capitol Police authority/personnel with congressional consent, require training/certification).
provisions.exact_change: Amend 3 U.S.C. §301 to read the quoted expanded text: retains existing delegation language and appends new clauses authorizing establishment of executive departments/agencies from existing authorities; suspension/transfer of statutory authorities and personnel; transfer/suspension of U.S. Capitol Police statutory authority and personnel with written consent of both principal congressional officers; and mandatory training/certification requirements.
provisions.evidence: “3 U.S. Code §301 - General authorization to delegate functions; publication of delegations is amended to read: The President of the United States is authorized to designate and empower...”
provisions.notes: THIS IS A REAL TEXTUAL AMENDMENT to a numbered USC section — must be an actual amend-existing-text, not a note. On PL 9-53's own repeal, this amendment's disposition must be resolved by the integrator.

provisions.text_summary: Amends 'The Employees Termination Notification Act of 2021' by adding Section 5 (Presidential Exemption for terminating political appointees).
provisions.target: prior 'Employees Termination Notification Act of 2021' (non-USC-numbered target)
provisions.exact_change: Add new SEC. 5 (Presidential Exemption): President exempt from the act re: termination of political appointees.
provisions.evidence: “The Employees Termination Notification Act of 2021 is amended by adding an additional section (section 5)... (a) The President... is exempt from the provisions of this act in relation to the termination of political appointees.”
provisions.notes: Target act not identified as a USC section; integrator must locate.

provisions.text_summary: Amends Section 5(c-d) of 'The Federal Metro Policing Act' (PL 9-60), striking and replacing with new text on administrative changes and MPD Executive Office Leadership composition.
provisions.exact_change: Strike Section 5(c-d) of the Federal Metro Policing Act and replace with the quoted text: (c) President/designee may provide administrative changes, prescribe logos/designs, establish divisions/bureaus/offices/chain of command; (d) MPD Executive Office Leadership = Chief, Deputy Chief, Assistant Chief, Commanders.
provisions.evidence: “Section 5 (c-d) of The Federal Metro Policing Act is stricken and shall now read as: (c) The President, or an authorized designee may provide for certain administrative changes...”
provisions.notes: Ordering oddity: 9-53 (seq 53) amends 9-60 (seq 60), which has a higher sequence — both in this batch. Flag for integrator; PL 9-60 is itself repealed (see 9-60).

provisions.text_summary: Recognizes the District of Columbia as a political subdivision of the Federal Government; Congress asserts authority over former municipal entities.
provisions.evidence: “The District of Columbia shall be recognized as a political subdivision of the Federal Government of the United States.”
provisions.notes: Two sections numbered 'SECTION 8' in source.

provisions.text_summary: Statutory definitions ('The Secretary', 'Principal officers', 'Political appointee').
provisions.evidence: “‘The Secretary’ refers to the Secretary of Commerce...”
provisions.notes: Duplicate SECTION 8 numbering in source.

provisions.text_summary: Severability; anti-supersession clause.
provisions.evidence: “This act shall be severable...”

provisions.text_summary: Immediate effective date on passage.
provisions.evidence: “This act shall immediately take effect upon passage.”

current_implementation.detail: Full-text dump + Trello link at t3/s1. Note says 'Operative amendments are integrated where determinable,' but the draft only added a note; the express amendment to 3 U.S.C. §301 (SEC. 5) was NOT performed as actual textual amendment. Placement at t3/s1 is generic; the §301 amendment target is t3/s301. Because PL 9-53 is itself repealed, the integrator must decide whether its §301 amendment survives or is unwound.

recommended_actions: Perform the actual textual amendment of 3 U.S.C. §301 per SEC. 5 (or, given PL 9-53's repeal, resolve whether the amendment is unwound) — do not leave it as a note.
Record express repeals of PL 7-40 and PL 3-22 (SEC. 2) as repeal-marking/history.
Record SEC. 6 and SEC. 7 amendments to the Employees Termination Notification Act and PL 9-60 as amendments/history; flag the 9-53→amends→9-60 ordering oddity.
Remove full-text dump and Trello link from rp-pl009053-codification; move the §301 change to t3/s301.
